reasoning: The Florida 2025 regular legislative session concluded on Friday, May 2, 2025. The bills specifically targeting citizenship verification for voters, such as HB 831, SB 1330, and HB 1381, all failed to pass and were indefinitely postponed or died in committee. Although another election-related bill, HB 1205, successfully passed and was signed into law (Ch. 2025-21), its provisions focus exclusively on limiting citizen-led constitutional amendments rather than establishing new voter citizenship verification requirements. Because the regular session is over and the relevant legislative vehicles have died, enacting this specific requirement before the July 1, 2025 deadline would necessitate an unexpected special legislative session. There are no indications that Governor DeSantis or legislative leaders plan to call a special session for this issue, so the likelihood of enactment by the deadline is exceedingly low.
